verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is a soluble protein. While it forms complexes with other IZUMO family members on sperm, its primary documented localization is to the acrosomal lumen (intracellular compartment), not the outer cell surface. Without its own membrane anchor, it cannot reach the extracellular face by an autonomous mechanism. The protein does not qualify for contextual surface accessibility despite being part of the sperm fertilization machinery.
